page.title: Plan a Romantic Proposal on a Marbella Sunset Cruise

data.meta_description: Plan a romantic proposal on a Marbella sunset cruise. Private boat, champagne, golden hour views of Puerto Banús. Book your perfect moment at sea.
data.summary: Discover how to plan a romantic proposal on a Marbella sunset cruise. From choosing the right boat to timing the golden hour, this guide covers everything you need for an unforgettable moment on the Costa del Sol.
key_facts: All charters include a licensed skipper, fuel, drinks, and snacks.
Sunset cruises typically depart 1.5–2 hours before sunset for optimal light.
Puerto Banús is the main departure point for our fleet.
Boats range from 6.4m (Speedboat) to 29m (Ferretti 94), accommodating 5–12 guests.
Champagne and custom decorations can be arranged on request.
The best sunset months are April–October, with the sun setting around 21:00 in summer.
Marbella's coastline offers calm seas and stunning backdrops like La Concha mountain.

faq.q: What is the best time for a sunset proposal cruise in Marbella?
faq.a: The ideal time to depart is 1.5 to 2 hours before sunset. In summer, sunset is around 21:00, so a 19:00 departure gives you time to enjoy the cruise, anchor at a scenic spot, and pop the question as the sky turns orange. Spring and autumn sunsets are earlier, typically 19:00–20:30.

faq.q: Can I bring my own decorations or champagne on board?
faq.a: Yes, you are welcome to bring personal decorations, flowers, or a special bottle. Our standard charter includes cava, but we can arrange premium champagne or specific flowers on request. Just let us know at booking so we can coordinate with the skipper.

faq.q: Which boat is best for a romantic proposal for two?
faq.a: For an intimate proposal, the Dubhe (8m, 5 pax) or the Speedboat (7m, 6 pax) are excellent choices. The Dubhe offers a cosy, private setting with a small cabin, while the Speedboat is open and sporty. Both depart from Puerto Banús and are ideal for two people.

faq.q: Do I need to be a sailor to charter a boat for a proposal?
faq.a: No, absolutely not. Every charter includes a licensed skipper who handles all navigation. You simply relax, enjoy the views, and focus on the moment. The skipper will also know the best quiet coves and sunset spots along the coast.

faq.q: What happens if the weather is bad on the day of the proposal?
faq.a: Safety is our priority. If conditions are unsafe, we will contact you to reschedule or offer a full refund. Marbella enjoys over 300 sunny days a year, so bad weather is rare, but we always monitor forecasts closely.

faq.q: How far in advance should I book a proposal cruise?
faq.a: We recommend booking at least 2–3 weeks in advance, especially for summer weekends and popular boats like the Astondoa 40 or Azimut 58 Flybridge. Last-minute bookings may be possible, but availability is limited. Contact us via WhatsApp for the fastest response.

faq.q: Can I arrange a photographer or musician on board?
faq.a: Yes, we can help coordinate a photographer to capture the moment discreetly, or a musician for live acoustic music. These services are arranged separately and subject to availability. Let us know your requirements during booking.

faq.q: Are there any hidden costs for a proposal cruise?
faq.a: No. Our prices are all-inclusive: licensed skipper, fuel, drinks (water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava), snacks, insurance, and 21% IVA. What you see is what you pay. Extra services like decorations or champagne are quoted upfront.

data.body_html:
Asking the most important question of your life deserves a setting that matches the moment. A Marbella sunset cruise romantic proposal combines the golden hour glow of the Costa del Sol with the intimacy of a private boat, creating a memory that will last a lifetime. Whether you choose a sleek motor yacht or a cosy speedboat, the backdrop of Puerto Banús, La Concha mountain, and the Mediterranean Sea sets the scene perfectly.

Why a Sunset Cruise Works for a Proposal

The psychology of a sunset proposal is simple: warm light flatters everyone, the sea breeze calms nerves, and the privacy of a boat means no interruptions. Marbella's coastline offers calm waters and stunning views, from the glamorous Puerto Banús to the quieter Cala del Faro. With over 300 sunny days a year, the odds of a perfect sunset are in your favour. Our all-inclusive charters take the stress out of planning — you just bring the ring.

Choosing the Right Boat for Your Proposal

Your boat choice depends on how many guests you want and the vibe you're after. For an intimate two-person proposal, the Dubhe (8m, 5 pax) offers a small cabin and private deck — ideal for a quiet moment. The Speedboat (7m, 6 pax) is sporty and open, perfect for couples who want to feel the sea air. If you're planning a surprise with a few close friends, the Astondoa 40 (12.5m, 9 pax) or Azimut 39 (12.5m, 11 pax) offer more space and a flybridge for panoramic views. For the ultimate luxury, the Mangusta 80 (24m, 12 pax) includes a jet ski and a dedicated crew to ensure everything runs smoothly. All boats depart from Puerto Banús.

Timing Your Sunset Cruise

Sunset times in Marbella vary by season. In summer (June–August), the sun sets around 21:00–21:30, so a 19:00 departure gives you two hours of cruising before the golden hour. In spring and autumn, sunset is earlier (19:00–20:30), so aim to depart 1.5 hours before. Your skipper knows the best anchor spots — often near Cala Cortés or off the Golden Mile — where the sun dips behind La Concha mountain. Always check the marine forecast, though we rarely cancel due to weather.

What’s Included in Your Charter

Every charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, and safety equipment. Spanish IVA (21%) is included in the price. For a proposal, we can arrange premium champagne, flowers, or a custom cake on request. The skipper can also help you time the moment — they'll slow the boat and point out the best view. Prices start from €749 for 2 hours on the Astondoa 40 or Azimut 39, up to €4,719 minimum 4 hours on the Mangusta 80 (which includes a jet ski). For other boats, contact us via WhatsApp for a quote.

Best Spots Along the Coast for a Proposal

Your skipper will navigate to one of several stunning locations depending on sea conditions and your preference. Cala del Faro offers a rocky cove with crystal-clear water and views back to Marbella. Cala Cortés is a quiet beach backed by cliffs, ideal for anchoring and popping the question. For a more glamorous backdrop, cruise past the superyachts of Puerto Banús and stop just offshore near the Río Verde estuary. If you want a longer cruise, head west towards Estepona or Sotogrande — both offer beautiful sunset views with fewer boats. The skipper will tailor the route to your wishes.

Practical Tips for a Flawless Proposal

Plan ahead: book at least 2–3 weeks in advance, especially for weekends and popular boats. Bring the ring in a secure case — the skipper can store it in the cabin if you want to surprise your partner. Dress in layers: even summer evenings can get breezy on the water. A light jacket or pashmina is a good idea. Consider a photographer — we can arrange one to capture the moment from a second boat or discreetly on board. Finally, don't forget to browse our full fleet and choose the boat that feels right for you.

What to Do After the Proposal

Once you've said yes, the cruise continues. Enjoy the remaining sunset light, toast with cava or champagne, and take in the twinkling lights of Marbella's coastline as darkness falls. Many couples choose to extend their charter into the evening, cruising back to Puerto Banús under the stars. We can arrange a dinner booking at a waterfront restaurant on the Golden Mile or in Puerto Banús itself. Just let us know — we're happy to help with recommendations.

Frequently asked questions

What is the best time for a sunset proposal cruise in Marbella?

The ideal time to depart is 1.5 to 2 hours before sunset. In summer, sunset is around 21:00, so a 19:00 departure gives you time to enjoy the cruise, anchor at a scenic spot, and pop the question as the sky turns orange. Spring and autumn sunsets are earlier, typically 19:00–20:30.

Can I bring my own decorations or champagne on board?

Yes, you are welcome to bring personal decorations, flowers, or a special bottle. Our standard charter includes cava, but we can arrange premium champagne or specific flowers on request. Just let us know at booking so we can coordinate with the skipper.

Which boat is best for a romantic proposal for two?

For an intimate proposal, the Dubhe (8m, 5 pax) or the Speedboat (7m, 6 pax) are excellent choices. The Dubhe offers a cosy, private setting with a small cabin, while the Speedboat is open and sporty. Both depart from Puerto Banús and are ideal for two people.

Do I need to be a sailor to charter a boat for a proposal?

No, absolutely not. Every charter includes a licensed skipper who handles all navigation. You simply relax, enjoy the views, and focus on the moment. The skipper will also know the best quiet coves and sunset spots along the coast.

What happens if the weather is bad on the day of the proposal?

Safety is our priority. If conditions are unsafe, we will contact you to reschedule or offer a full refund. Marbella enjoys over 300 sunny days a year, so bad weather is rare, but we always monitor forecasts closely.

How far in advance should I book a proposal cruise?

We recommend booking at least 2–3 weeks in advance, especially for summer weekends and popular boats like the Astondoa 40 or Azimut 58 Flybridge. Last-minute bookings may be possible, but availability is limited. Contact us via WhatsApp for the fastest response.

Can I arrange a photographer or musician on board?

Yes, we can help coordinate a photographer to capture the moment discreetly, or a musician for live acoustic music. These services are arranged separately and subject to availability. Let us know your requirements during booking.

Are there any hidden costs for a proposal cruise?

No. Our prices are all-inclusive: licensed skipper, fuel, drinks (water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava), snacks, insurance, and 21% IVA. What you see is what you pay. Extra services like decorations or champagne are quoted upfront.
